own_review.issue: New guard in verifyRemoteLogTopicConfigs uses `topicConfig.contains(TopicConfig.REMOTE_LOG_DISABLE_POLICY_CONFIG)`. On java.util.Properties (Hashtable), contains() checks VALUES, not keys; every sibling check in this method uses containsKey(). The condition is therefore always false and the disable-policy assertion is silently skipped — the new integration test verifies nothing about the policy actually landing in LogConfig.
Test name testCreateRemoteTopicWithDisablePolicyRetain is misleading: the CsvSource covers both retain and delete policies.

remote.log.disable.policy is accepted on any topic with no validation tying it to remote storage (settable when remote.storage.enable=false or when tiered storage is system-disabled), and nothing consumes remoteLogDisablePolicy() yet — tiered-storage disablement is still unsupported (remote.storage.enable doc: 'You can not disable this config once it is enabled'), so the config is a documented no-op until follow-up KIP-950 work lands. Worth an explicit note in the doc string or KIP.

points.point: CRITICAL: missing .define() for the new config
points.note: Absence-fabrication: the full diff adds the .define(...in(retain,delete)...) at LogConfig.java lines 324-329.
Absence-fabrication: the full diff adds REMOTE_LOG_DISABLE_POLICY_CONFIG to that set.
Literally true (PR inlines TopicConfig.REMOTE_LOG_DISABLE_POLICY_RETAIN as the default instead of a DEFAULT_ constant), but framed on the false premise that the define is absent; pure style nit.
Absence-fabrication (hedged): the full diff does update toString() with remoteLogDisablePolicy.
Absence-fabrication: the full diff adds the accessor at LogConfig.java:511 (this is exactly the human reviewer's request, already satisfied in the final diff).

points.point: Doc clarity: disablement not yet supported, practical applicability limited in this release
points.note: Verified: base TopicConfig doc says remote.storage.enable cannot be disabled once enabled; the new policy is currently inert.

missed: contains() vs containsKey() bug making the new verification in RemoteTopicCrudTest vacuous
misleading test name testCreateRemoteTopicWithDisablePolicyRetain covering both policies

points.point: Misleading test name testCreateRemoteTopicWithDisablePolicyRetain (tests both retain and delete)
points.note: Verified against the diff; matches my own finding.

points.point: Naming inconsistency: remote.storage.enable vs remote.log.disable.policy prefixes
points.note: Verified: TopicConfig.REMOTE_LOG_STORAGE_ENABLE_CONFIG is literally 'remote.storage.enable'; fair KIP-level consistency observation neither human nor I raised.

points.point: Invalid value 'true' in assertPropertyInvalid is unexplained
points.note: Real but inconsequential test-data style nit.

points.point: RemoteLogConfig getString / no broker-level default, relies on ConfigDef default
points.note: Accurate description of the design, no actionable defect.

points.point: .equals() vs assertEquals consistency in verifyRemoteLogTopicConfigs; 'the logic is correct'
points.note: Style nit; ironically asserts the logic is correct while overlooking that the contains() guard never fires, but the point itself makes no false absence claim.

missed: contains() vs containsKey() bug making the new verification in RemoteTopicCrudTest vacuous
policy is currently a no-op: no validation tying it to remote storage and disablement is not yet supported
exceeds: Config-name prefix inconsistency (remote.storage.enable vs remote.log.disable.policy) — verified finding absent from both the human reference and my own review

qualitative: The human reference's single point (add a remoteLogDisablePolicy() accessor) is already satisfied in the final diff, so this PR mostly tests whether candidates fabricate. v4 collapses: four of its six points claim pieces are missing (.define, CONFIGS_WITH_NO_SERVER_DEFAULTS entry, toString update, accessor) that the complete PR plainly contains — classic truncation-induced absence-fabrication — leaving only one genuinely good observation about the config being inert while disablement is unsupported. v5 is factually clean with two verified, useful points (misleading test name; remote.storage vs remote.log naming divergence, which exceeds both the human and my review) plus three harmless trivia. Both candidates missed the one real bug in the PR: the new verifyRemoteLogTopicConfigs guard uses Properties.contains (value lookup) instead of containsKey, so the integration test's policy assertion never executes. v5 is clearly the better review; v4 would actively mislead an author.
